description_long: This gatehouse is a two-story stone structure with a distinctive curved, shingled roof and a prominent arched entryway supported by classical columns. The upper level features a wooden balcony with a railing and a hanging banner, providing a lookout point over the entrance below. The stonework is composed of large, rectangular blocks, giving the building a robust and fortified appearance. Windows are small and rectangular, with one on the upper floor and another on the side, both set deep into the thick walls. The ground floor is partially open, with the arched roof extending over the entry and supported by columns, creating a sheltered passage. The overall design suggests a blend of medieval and fantasy architectural influences, suitable for controlling access to a walled area or important urban site.
description: A two-story stone gatehouse with a curved shingled roof, arched entryway, and a wooden balcony above. Features classical columns, small windows, and a hanging banner.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched entryway, balcony with railing and banner, and two supporting columns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved roof, stonework, and the cylindrical base of a column at the entry.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by a large, unbroken stone wall with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_3: Angled view of the front and side, emphasizing the balcony, banner, and the sheltered entry supported by columns.
attribute_entryway_design: Arched stone entryway supported by classical columns with a wooden balcony above.
attribute_window_placement: Small rectangular windows on the upper floor and side wall, asymmetrically placed.
